figures.caption: Table 1: Main results on LongMemEval-S and LoCoMo. Token cost is measured in thousands, and time cost in seconds. – indicates no value for the metric. N/A indicates unavailable values due to the absence of a public implementation. Bold indicates the best value, underline indicates the secondbest. For LoCoMo, AccNonAdv and AccAdv denote accuracy on non-adversarial and adversarial questions, respectively. “w/o conv-26” reports results after excluding the only LoCoMo conversation instance used for training the full DeferMem distiller. DEFERMEM† denotes DEFERMEM-LCM for LongMemEval-S and DEFERMEM-LME for LoCoMo.
Table 5: Training and decoding configurations for the DeferMem distiller.
Table 6: Question-type breakdown on LoCoMo after excluding conv-26. We report accuracy (%) for each method. Bold indicates the best result, and underline indicates the second-best result.
Figure 3: Distribution of the number of distilled evidence entries returned by DeferMem.

Table 2: Hyperparameter analysis of the segment-link retriever on LongMemEval-S and LoCoMo. We vary the segment boundary threshold τ1 and the segment-link threshold τ2. Metrics include Recall-01, Recall, and the average number of retrieved tokens.
Figure 2: DeferMem framework: (1) a segment-link retriever produces high-recall candidates, and (2) a DistillPO-trained memory distiller converts them into query-conditioned evidence.
Table 3: Additional analysis results. In (a) and (c), LoCoMo excludes conv-26. In (a), ∆ denotes the accuracy drop relative to full DeferMem. In (b), LME-M denotes LongMemEval-M.
Table 9: Reward components used by DistillPO.
